title: The Leshy: The Voice That Wears Your Mother's Mouth

shots.narration: If you hear your name in the woods, don't answer. Not the first time. Not the third.
shots.prompt: A vast forest at dusk, pale birch trunks interwoven with dark towering conifers, endless trunks receding into near-black shadow, a single warm amber shaft of light between them, heavy ink linework, watercolor washes, paper grain, cinematic 16:9, Deep Root Lore style

shots.narration: The old ones in the villages had a rule for the forest. Keep the sun on the same shoulder the whole way through. If it moves to the other one, and you never turned — you're already his.
shots.prompt: A lone hooded traveler seen from behind, faceless in deep shadow, small against a mixed forest of towering dark conifers and pale birch trunks, one shoulder rimmed in warm amber light, no visible face, ink and watercolor, chiaroscuro, cinematic 16:9, Deep Root Lore style

shots.narration: His. The Leshy's. Lord of the woods, in the old Slavic tongue. The forest doesn't belong to the tsar or the church out here. It belongs to him.
shots.prompt: A colossal humanoid silhouette formed from bark, moss and antler-like branches, eyes two faint green embers deep in the canopy, backlit, mostly shadow, ink linework, watercolor, cinematic 16:9, Deep Root Lore style

shots.narration: They say he can stand tall as the oldest oak, his hair the crown of the canopy. Then, in a breath, shrink small enough to hide under a mushroom cap. Same creature. Whatever size the moment needs.
shots.prompt: A colossal antlered forest humanoid taller than the pines, body of bark, moss and shadow, looming over the treetops against a pale amber sky, NOT winged, no wings, ink and watercolor, warm amber accents, cinematic 16:9, Deep Root Lore style

shots.narration: There's one way to be sure it's him. Look at the ground beside him at noon. The trees throw shadows. The stones throw shadows. He throws nothing.
shots.prompt: A tall antlered forest figure of bark and shadow standing in a bright midday clearing, every tree casting a long dark shadow but the figure casting NONE, unsettling bare lit ground where its shadow should be, no wings, ink and watercolor, cinematic 16:9, Deep Root Lore style

shots.narration: And the voice. This is the part they lower their own voices to tell. He listens. He learns you. Then he calls with the mouth of someone you'd run toward.
shots.prompt: Close on a woman's ear in warm candlelight, a faint mouth-shaped hollow in the tree bark just behind her, lips parted, near-black background, ink linework, watercolor, cinematic 16:9, Deep Root Lore style

shots.narration: Your mother's voice. Your child's. A friend who died three winters back, calling like nothing happened.
shots.prompt: A single gnarled hand reaching out from behind a dark tree trunk into a shaft of warm amber light, only the hand and forearm visible, the rest lost in black shadow, an eerie beckoning lure, no face and no figure, ink and watercolor, chiaroscuro, cinematic 16:9, Deep Root Lore style

shots.narration: He doesn't chase. He never chases. He just moves the forest around you.
shots.prompt: High aerial view looking straight down on a lone hooded traveler on a winding forest path, the surrounding trees subtly rearranging into a spiral, faint looping footprints, deep forest not a village, warm amber path glow, ink and watercolor, cinematic 16:9, Deep Root Lore style

shots.narration: You pass a fallen birch with a hollow in it. You keep walking. An hour later — the same birch. Same hollow. Your own boot-prints going the wrong way.
shots.prompt: A distinctive fallen birch with a dark knot-hole, fresh boot prints in the mud leading past it and returning, low warm light, deep shadow, ink linework, watercolor, cinematic 16:9, Deep Root Lore style

shots.narration: There was a man from a village near the Pripyat marshes. Went out for firewood. This is how they told it after.
shots.prompt: A bearded peasant man in a rough coat with an axe over his shoulder, stepping into a dense treeline at golden hour, home a small warm smudge behind him, ink and watercolor, cinematic 16:9, Deep Root Lore style

shots.narration: Good axe. Knew the woods since he was a boy. He'd walked that path more times than he had years.
shots.prompt: A bearded peasant man walking away down a forest path at golden hour, the same worn short-handled firewood axe resting over one shoulder (not a large battle-axe), seen from behind and slightly to the side, weathered coat, hands relaxed at his sides and not the focus, tall trees, heavy ink linework, watercolor washes, paper grain, cinematic 16:9, Deep Root Lore style

shots.narration: Halfway in, the birds stopped. Not flew off. Stopped. Like someone had shut a door on the sound.
shots.prompt: A hushed forest interior, a single startled bird frozen mid-flight, everything unnaturally still, muted washes, deep shadow between trunks, ink linework, cinematic 16:9, Deep Root Lore style

shots.narration: Then a voice from up the path. His wife. Calling his name, easy, like she'd come to fetch him for supper.
shots.prompt: A woman's warm silhouette standing far up a sunlit forest path, apron and headscarf, one hand raised, her face unreadable in shadow, ink and watercolor, amber glow, cinematic 16:9, Deep Root Lore style

shots.narration: He almost answered. Then he remembered. He'd left her at the loom that morning, three miles back. She had no reason to be in these trees.
shots.prompt: Split memory image: the same woman seated at a wooden loom in a warm cottage window, threads half-woven, contrasted with the dark forest edge, ink and watercolor, chiaroscuro, cinematic 16:9, Deep Root Lore style

shots.narration: The voice called again. Sweeter. Closer. And it got her name for him just slightly wrong — the pet name only she used. Almost right. Almost.
shots.prompt: The woman's silhouette now much nearer on the path, her outline subtly wrong — too tall, fingers too long, edges bleeding into bark, ink linework, watercolor, near-black, cinematic 16:9, Deep Root Lore style

shots.narration: He looked down. Bright sun overhead, his own shadow long on the moss. Hers — nothing. Grass, lit clean, right where a shadow ought to lie.
shots.prompt: Low forest-floor angle: a bearded man's long dark shadow stretching across sunlit moss, and right beside it a tall figure's bare feet casting no shadow at all on the glowing grass, the man's cast shadow a plain featureless dark silhouette with no facial features, ink and watercolor, cinematic 16:9, Deep Root Lore style

shots.narration: That's when the path stopped agreeing to be a path.
shots.prompt: A forest trail dissolving and folding in on itself, trees leaning inward like a closing throat, warm amber light narrowing to a pinpoint, ink and watercolor, cinematic 16:9, Deep Root Lore style

shots.narration: Every direction was the same tree. He walked an hour and crossed his own tracks. Walked another and crossed them again. The forest had folded him inside its own pocket.
shots.prompt: A disoriented man turning in circles in an identical-looking clearing, faint spiral of overlapping footprints beneath him, repeating trunks in every direction, ink and watercolor, cinematic 16:9, Deep Root Lore style

shots.narration: Now. Here's what his grandmother taught him, and what saved him. When the woods turn on you, you turn on the woods. Everything you're wearing — off, and back on, inside-out.
shots.prompt: A bearded peasant man crouched low among forest trees and ferns in a dim beam of light, pulling his coat off with the rough seams turned outward, focused and afraid, outdoors in the woods not indoors, ink and watercolor, chiaroscuro, cinematic 16:9, Deep Root Lore style

shots.narration: Boots on the wrong feet. Coat seams to the sky. You make yourself into a thing his trick can't hold. A wrong shape in a spell built for right ones.
shots.prompt: The man standing again, clothes visibly inside-out and boots swapped, awkward and deliberate, the folding forest recoiling slightly around him, ink linework, watercolor, cinematic 16:9, Deep Root Lore style

shots.narration: And you don't leave empty-handed. You leave something for him. Bread and salt on a stump, at a crossroads, wherever two ways meet. Payment for passage. A guest's manners in another lord's hall.
shots.prompt: A tree stump at a forest crossroads with a torn hunk of dark bread and a small mound of white salt on a cloth, lit only by a warm amber glow with no candle or flame visible, reverent stillness, ink and watercolor, cinematic 16:9, Deep Root Lore style

shots.narration: He left the bread. Turned his coat. And he did the last thing — the hardest one. He stopped listening for her voice.
shots.prompt: The man walking away with hands over his ears, the sweet familiar silhouette behind him fading back into the trees, unanswered, ink and watercolor, chiaroscuro, cinematic 16:9, Deep Root Lore style

shots.narration: The birds came back first. Then the light thinned into evening, honest evening, the kind that moves. And the treeline opened onto his own field.
shots.prompt: The forest edge parting to reveal a rye field at dusk, a small cottage with one warm lit window in the distance, birds scattering into an open sky, ink and watercolor, amber glow, cinematic 16:9, Deep Root Lore style

shots.narration: His wife met him at the door. She said she'd never once called for him. She'd been at the loom the whole day. Of course she had.
shots.prompt: A woman opening the heavy wooden door of a Slavic log-cabin izba at night, snow-dusted pines outside, warm firelight spilling from within, a bearded man's dark exhausted silhouette standing on the threshold seen from behind, a wooden loom inside, European folk cottage, NOT a bamboo hut, no palm trees, ink and watercolor, chiaroscuro, cinematic 16:9, Deep Root Lore style

shots.narration: He was gone one afternoon. When he came out, the axe he'd carried in was rusted through, like it had waited on a stump for forty years.
shots.prompt: Close on the axe resting against the doorframe, its head bloomed with deep orange rust, cobwebbed, though the handle is fresh, warm candlelight, ink and watercolor, cinematic 16:9, Deep Root Lore style

shots.narration: So. If you walk where the old forest still stands, keep the sun on one shoulder. Turn your coat before you set out, not after. Leave your bread at the crossing.
shots.prompt: A wide dusk view of a dark ancient forest wall meeting an open road, a small figure pausing at the treeline turning their coat inside-out, offering stump nearby, ink and watercolor, cinematic 16:9, Deep Root Lore style

shots.narration: And if something up the path calls you by a name only love should know — look at the ground. If nothing dark lies where it's standing, then whatever loves you is not there. Keep walking. Don't answer.
shots.prompt: Final shot: deep in the trees a familiar human silhouette waits in a shaft of pale light, casting absolutely no shadow on the bright grass beneath it (the lit ground unbroken), near-black forest closing around, eerie and still, ink and watercolor, cinematic 16:9, Deep Root Lore style
